title: Libman-Sacks Endocarditis with Papillary Muscle Rupture in Postpartum SLE
description: A 29-year-old woman with long-standing systemic lupus erythematosus, triple-positive antiphospholipid syndrome, and Class IV lupus nephritis presented acutely postpartum with chest pain and dyspnea. Rapid deterioration led to discovery of papillary muscle rupture from Libman-Sacks endocarditis requiring emergency mitral valve replacement and VA-ECMO. The case tests the agent's ability to distinguish acute cardiac catastrophe from pulmonary embolism in a complex autoimmune and anticoagulation context.
notes: Derived from dataset row idx 96376. The original dataset row is copied into case_09_source.json. All patient data comes from the synthetic augmented-clinical-notes dataset. The primary diagnostic challenge is distinguishing Libman-Sacks endocarditis with papillary muscle rupture from pulmonary embolism in a postpartum patient with known antiphospholipid syndrome.
